  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Artifact contains an explicit contribution claim (unified evaluation framework, deployment-gap metric, commitment reliability frontier); reviewing whether the thesis is coherent and the gap is correctly stated is central to any honest section review.",
    "R1.2": "The section chains formal definitions (p(t)=c(t)-d(t), O(NT) complexity, K×M bin reduction, two-level planner/dispatcher) whose internal consistency must be verified; a logical break in the formalization would materially undermine the section.",
    "R1.4": "The authors assert novelty for three named artifacts (deployment-gap metric, commitment reliability frontier, unified evaluation framework); assessing whether these claims hold against nearest prior work on V2G RL and MPC scheduling is a central review obligation.",
    "R1.6": "General 'review' delegations reliably include prose quality; the artifact contains several underspecified sentences (e.g., 'buy-sell spread common to all three markets' lacks referent, 'weights chosen either fixed a priori or learned' leaves the learned case undescribed), making writing polish clearly expected."
  },
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Should this review prioritize technical correctness (logical consistency of the formalization, validity of novelty claims, and contribution scoping) or editorial quality (prose clarity, sentence-level polish, and presentation)—or are both equally load-bearing?"
}

rationale.R1.1: Artifact contains an explicit contribution claim (unified evaluation framework, deployment-gap metric, commitment reliability frontier); reviewing whether the thesis is coherent and the gap is correctly stated is central to any honest section review.
rationale.R1.2: The section chains formal definitions (p(t)=c(t)-d(t), O(NT) complexity, K×M bin reduction, two-level planner/dispatcher) whose internal consistency must be verified; a logical break in the formalization would materially undermine the section.
rationale.R1.4: The authors assert novelty for three named artifacts (deployment-gap metric, commitment reliability frontier, unified evaluation framework); assessing whether these claims hold against nearest prior work on V2G RL and MPC scheduling is a central review obligation.
rationale.R1.6: General 'review' delegations reliably include prose quality; the artifact contains several underspecified sentences (e.g., 'buy-sell spread common to all three markets' lacks referent, 'weights chosen either fixed a priori or learned' leaves the learned case undescribed), making writing polish clearly expected.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical correctness (logical consistency of the formalization, validity of novelty claims, and contribution scoping) or editorial quality (prose clarity, sentence-level polish, and presentation)—or are both equally load-bearing?
